question.text: Scenario: Arun Kumar is analyzing Reliance Industries (RIL) stock using multiple technical indicators. Current observations: (1) RIL CMP: Rs.2,850. (2) 50-day SMA: Rs.2,780 (rising). 200-day SMA: Rs.2,650 (rising). (3) The 50-day SMA crossed above the 200-day SMA one month ago. (4) RSI: 58. (5) Bollinger Bands: Upper band Rs.2,950, Middle band Rs.2,780, Lower band Rs.2,610. Price is between middle and upper band. (6) Strong support at Rs.2,600 (tested 3 times in last 6 months). (7) Resistance at Rs.2,950 (tested twice). (8) Volume has been above average on recent up-days. (9) MACD is above the signal line and above the zero baseline. Question 2 of 5: RIL's current position relative to moving averages (price above 50 SMA above 200 SMA) indicates:

answer.text: A bearish alignment suggesting shorting opportunities
A bullish alignment: Price > 50 SMA > 200 SMA with both MAs rising confirms a healthy uptrend
A sideways market with no clear direction
The stock is oversold and due for a correction

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When Price > 50 SMA > 200 SMA and both MAs are rising, it's the most bullish alignment possible. All timeframes confirm the uptrend. **The Trap:** This is the ideal bullish structure. Bearish would be the reverse (Price < 50 < 200). **Cross-Exam Context:** Ch 15 NISM XV. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This alignment using 13/21/34 EMAs from the Fibonacci series would show: Price > 13 EMA > 21 EMA > 34 EMA in a bull market.
